summary: David Lang, a physics professor, is running late for his lecture at Cornell after discovering that fifteen minutes of his morning have vanished inexplicably. He joins his colleagues Sweeney, Naomi, and Bardon on the walk up East Hill, recovering his normal banter, but experiences a strange physical pressure and low-frequency hum near a seam in the paving stones.
outline: David Lang wakes up late and realizes 15 minutes have vanished from his watch.
David scrambles to catch up with his colleagues walking up the slope to Feynman Hall.
David engages in academic banter with Sweeney, Naomi, and Bardon, masking his disorientation.
David experiences a sudden, violent chest pressure and low-frequency vibration at a paving seam.
Naomi notices David is sweating and not okay, but David insists on continuing to his lecture.
analysis: This opening scene establishes David's obsession with perfect timing and control, contrasted immediately by a breakdown in physical time (the missing 15 minutes). The low-frequency hum (68.48 Hz) introduces the central motif of resonance as a disruptive force that behaves like a physical weight or structural vibration. The seam in the paving stones serves as a physical threshold between ordinary reality and the strange aperture of Project ARGO.
